news_bbc.title: 'We give up to £400': How much should you gift at a wedding?
news_bbc.summary: Wedding lists are being replaced by cash requests, but guests are divided over how much to give.

news_bbc.title: Burnham says there is some room for movement on tax
news_bbc.summary: But the likely next PM says he will stick to Labour's pledges to not raise VAT, income tax or national insurance.

news_bbc.title: Pubs allowed to stay open until 5am on Monday for England Mexico match
news_bbc.summary: The government had initially said it would not relax licensing laws further for the World Cup.

news_bbc.title: How millions will have eaten takeaway 'lamb' kebabs made of goat meat and skin
news_bbc.summary: Millions are likely to have eaten "lamb" kebabs that were actually made with goat, skin and fat.

news_bbc.title: Instagram running ads promoting child sexual abuse material in India, BBC finds
news_bbc.summary: The ads use terms including “rape” and “child video” and link to content on the messaging app Telegram.

news_bbc.title: AI is 'not smart' so what's next in artificial intelligence?
news_bbc.summary: Leading AI researcher Yan LeCun has a start-up which is developing a more flexible AI system.

news_bbc.title: Security staff to go on strike at Aberdeen Airport
news_bbc.summary: The strikes, involving baggage screening staff, are set to begin on Monday with the Unite union warning of "significant delays".

news_bbc.title: Ryanair warns of 'queue chaos' from new EU border system
news_bbc.summary: The airline calls on European governments to postpone the EES system until after the summer holiday period.
